front: Novel: the protagonist invented the academic field of Hitler Studies but doesn't know German; set at the College-on-the-Hill
Novel: Babette takes the experimental drug Dylar to overcome her fear of death, obtained through an affair with Willie Mink (Mr. Gray)
Novel: the 'Airborne Toxic Event' involving Nyodene D.; SIMUVAC uses real disasters to prepare for simulations
Novel: Murray Jay Siskind lectures on Elvis's mother simultaneously with the protagonist's Hitler lecture; they visit the 'most photographed barn in America'
Novel: opens with the 'Shot Heard Round the World' prologue originally published as 'Pafko at the Wall'; protagonist is a waste management executive
Novel: a baseball caught by Cotter Martin, stolen by his father Manx, traced by Marvin Lundy, and bought by the protagonist
Novel: J. Edgar Hoover fascinated by Bruegel's Triumph of Death; Klara Sax attends a private Zapruder film screening
Novel: depicts the life of Lee Harvey Oswald; the archivist Nicholas Branch reconstructs the central event; compares the Warren Commission report to Ulysses
Novel: reclusive author Bill Gray tries to rescue a Swiss poet held by terrorists in Beirut; opens with a Unification Church mass wedding at Yankee Stadium
Novel: billionaire Eric Packer spends a day crossing New York in a limousine to get a haircut; kills his bodyguard Torval
Novel: filmmaker Jim Finley documents Iraq War architect Richard Elster; features Psycho slowed down to last 24 hours
Novel: Keith Neudecker survives 9/11; has an affair with Florence Givens despite being married to estranged Lianne; their son fears the imaginary 'Bill Lawton'
Novel: Jackie Gleason vomits on Frank Sinatra's shoes at the Shot Heard Round the World game; J. Edgar Hoover fascinated by Bruegel's Triumph of Death in Life magazine reprint
Novel: Warren Commission report described as 'the megaton novel that James Joyce would have written if he'd moved to Iowa City and lived to be a hundred'
Novel: risk analyst John Axton and archaeologist Owen Brademas investigate a cult that kills victims whose initials match town names; set in Athens
Novel: reporter Moll Robbins searches for a pornographic film of Adolf Hitler
